label: double_spring_mass
objective: Understand how two identical springs create net restoring force Fnet = -2kx around equilibrium O.

keyLabels.description: Displacement of mass from equilibrium O.
Combined restoring force from both springs.
Spring constant of each identical spring.
Ideal case: damping due to friction is ignored.

explainTop: At equilibrium O, left and right spring effects balance. When displaced by x, both springs exert restoring force toward O.
explainBottom: Because both springs are identical, restoring effects add to give Fnet = -(k + k)x = -2kx.

checkpointQuestions.prompt: Can you identify the instant when the mass is back near equilibrium O (x close to 0)?
checkpointQuestions.success: Correct: near O, displacement and net restoring force magnitude are near zero.
checkpointQuestions.hint: Track the x badge and watch for the mass crossing the center guide.

takeaway: Both spring forces are restoring and point toward O.
Net restoring force is proportional to displacement: Fnet = -2kx.
The system oscillates about equilibrium under ideal undamped assumptions.
